zh.lifeSummary: Laura de Vere是Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford和Countess Hawise de Quincy的女兒，出生在英國英格蘭奧可福爾奧可福德。她於1260年與Reginald d'Argentine結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1292年在英國英格蘭賀特福爾格亞特 維蒙德利，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可諾維其。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name, mostly from Vair in Ancenis, named with Gaulish ver(n)- ‘alder’. This was the probable home in the 11th century of the Breton lord Aubrey (Albericus) de Ver, who followed William the Conqueror to England and founded an influential family who held the earldom of Oxford until 1703. In a few cases the surname could be from other places in France, such as Ver in Manche and Calvados, which have the same etymology as Vair.
French (mainy Vendée; Veré): variant of Vairé, from Old French vairie ‘variegated, spotted’, applied as a nickname for someone who has freckles.

nameMeanings.name: Laura
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of the Late Latin male name Laurus ‘laurel’. St Laura was a 9th-century Spanish nun who met her death in a cauldron of molten lead. Laura is also the name of the woman addressed in the love poetry of the Italian poet Petrarch ( Francesco Petrarca , 1304–74 ), and it owes much of its subsequent popularity to this. There have been various speculations about her identity, but it has not been established with any certainty. He first met her in 1327 while living in Avignon, and she died of the plague in 1348 . The popularity of the given name in the English-speaking world has endured since the 19th century, when it was probably imported from Italy.

en.lifeSummary: Laura de Vere was born in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford and Countess Hawise de Quincy. She married Reginald d'Argentine in 1260. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1292, in Great Wymondley, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Laura de Vere est née à Oxford, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fille de Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford et Countess Hawise de Quincy. Elle a épousé Reginald d'Argentine en 1260. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1292, à Great Wymondley, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Laura de VereはイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアオクスフォードでSir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of OxfordとCountess Hawise de Quincyの娘として生まれた。1260年にReginald d'Argentineと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1292年にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアグレアト ウイモンドリーで死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクノーウィチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Laura de Vere(은)는 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스퍼드에서 Sir Hugh de Vere 4th Earl of Oxford(와)과 Countess Hawise de Quincy의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1260년 Reginald d'Argentine(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1292년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 그리트 윈이몽리에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 노위치에 묻혔다.
